verses.commentary: The focus now shifts to the next generation.
The story of Joseph begins.
Visible favoritism. Jacob repeats his father's mistake (25:28).
They hated him. Open hostility.
Joseph's lack of wisdom compounds the problem.
He should have kept it private.
Dream content. Binding sheaves. Clear symbolism of authority and submission.
The implication is clear: Joseph will rule over them. Their hate intensifies. Dreams reveal God's plan, but sharing them shows Joseph's immaturity.
Another dream. The symbolism is even more explicit.
Father rebuked him. Rachel is already dead (35:19), making the 'mother' reference puzzling, perhaps Leah or prophetic.
Brothers jealous. Jacob remembers. Like Mary pondering Jesus' words (Luke 2:19). Wondering if God is speaking.
Shechem, site of earlier violence (chapter 34). Ominous location.
Here I am (hineni, הִנֵּנִי). Obedient response.
Go see if all is well. Bring word.
Providence works through unnamed people. This man redirects Joseph to his destiny.
Joseph persists in his mission. Obedience even into danger.
The brothers have moved on. About 15 miles further north to Dothan.
The coat makes him visible from far away.
Here comes that dreamer (ba'al hachalomot, בַּעַל הַחֲלֹמוֹת), master of dreams. Mocking tone.
Let's kill him. Throw in cistern.
Trying to redeem himself after his sin (35:22).
Don't shed blood. Throw in cistern. Compromise to save him.
The ornate robe. Humiliation begins.
He can't escape or die quickly.
Sat to eat. Going to Egypt.
Judah's practical question: what profit in fratricide? Better to sell him. Pragmatic evil replaces passionate evil.
His brothers agreed. Let's sell him. Brothers agreed. Selling him as compromise between murder and release.
Midianite merchants. Pulled Joseph out. Taken to Egypt. Ishmaelites and Midianites used interchangeably (same region).
Joseph not there. Tore his clothes. Grief and panic.
Where can I turn? Reuben's distress.
Got Joseph's robe. Slaughtered goat. Dipped in blood.
Examine it to see whether it is your son's robe. Examine it. They don't directly lie but lead Jacob to wrong conclusion.
He recognized it. My son's robe. Animal devoured him. Torn to pieces. Jacob used goat skins to deceive Isaac (27:16), now goat blood deceives him.
Jacob tore clothes. Put on sackcloth. Mourned many days. Deep grief.
He refused comfort. Father wept.
To Potiphar. Pharaoh's official. Captain of guard. Scene shift. Potiphar introduced.
